title: Fixed-Rate Crypto Swap: How a Locked Quote Changes the Transaction
keyQuestions: What is a fixed-rate crypto swap?
What does a fixed quote actually lock during a crypto swap?
Why does the quote window matter in a fixed-rate swap?
Does a fixed rate make the swap faster?
When is fixed rate better than floating rate?
What can still go wrong after a fixed quote is created?
quickSteps: Prepare the sending wallet and receiving wallet before opening the fixed quote.
Check the source asset, destination asset, network, amount, minimum, and receiving address against the quote screen.
Read the quote timer as a condition of the offer, not as a decoration.
Send only when the wallet’s final transaction details match the fixed quote.
Stop and create a fresh quote if the timer, network, amount, or address no longer matches.

issueRouting.start: The user wants a predictable crypto swap result and needs to decide whether a fixed-rate quote fits the transaction.

branches.if: The receiving amount must stay predictable
then: Choose the fixed-rate model before creating the quote.
Prepare both wallets before the timer starts.
Send only when the quote details match the wallet transaction.

branches.if: The quote timer is low or already expired
then: Do not rush the transaction into unclear conditions.
Let the old quote expire if the wallet is not ready.
Create a fresh quote and review the route again.

branches.if: The asset, network, amount, or address does not match
then: Correct the wallet or route details first.
Use a new quote only after the transaction facts match.

riskNotes: A fixed rate locks the quote under stated conditions; it does not fix late deposits, wrong networks, or wrong addresses.
The quote timer is part of the fixed-rate offer and should not be treated as a visual detail.
A token ticker alone is not enough because the same ticker can exist on different networks.
Wallet fees can change the final amount sent, so the actual outgoing amount must be checked before confirming.
Support requests should use narrow public transaction facts, not seed phrases, private keys, backups, or remote access.
